series.note: Iran war begins — immediate price shock
National avg tops 200 cpl for first time
Govt announces excise halving from April 1
Federal government announced halving of fuel excise by 26.3 cpl for three months effective 1 April 2026, following National Cabinet meeting on 30 March amid Middle East conflict-driven price surge.
sources: ACCC Weekly Fuel Price Monitoring (27 Mar 2026)
AIP Weekly Petrol Prices Report (w/e 29 Mar 2026)

series.note: Fuel excise halved from 52.6 to 26.3 cpl on 1 April but most bowser stock still taxed at old rate; only partial pass-through on day 2, combined with Brent sliding toward $101 on Strait of Hormuz de-escalation hopes.
sources: AIP Weekly Petrol Prices Report (week ending 29 Mar 2026, published 30 Mar)
ACCC Weekly Fuel Price Monitoring Report (27 Mar 2026)

series.note: Fuel excise cut (26.3 cpl) continuing to flow through to bowsers but offset by Brent crude surging 8% on April 2 after Trump's national address signalled prolonged Iran conflict; ACCC warns retailers to pass on full savings.
sources: ACCC weekly fuel price monitoring (April 3 2026 media release)

timeline.event: Iran-Israel tensions reach critical point; US issues stark warning over Strait of Hormuz
Iran launches coordinated strikes — war officially begins; Hormuz threatened
Brent crude surges 11% to USD $96/bbl on opening of hostilities
Australian bowser prices jump 10–12 cpl overnight at major city stations
Iranian navy seizes two oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz
Brent crude reaches USD $104/bbl; Goldman Sachs raises forecast to $130
Australian national average petrol tops 200 cpl for the first time since 2022
US and allies impose sweeping oil sanctions on Iran; exports halted
Brent crude hits USD $112/bbl — highest since mid-2022 energy crisis
NRMA and RACQ call on federal government for emergency fuel excise suspension
OPEC+ emergency meeting fails to agree on output increase; Brent holds above $110
Australian government announces emergency fuel relief package review
Diesel national average tops 300 cpl — trucking industry warns of supply chain impact
Federal government announces fuel excise halved (52.6 → 26.3 cpl) effective April 1
Brent crude reaches USD $119/bbl as Hormuz shipping insurance costs triple
Treasury Laws Amendment (Doubling Penalties for ACCC Enforcement) Bill 2026 passed, raising maximum fines for fuel-sector misconduct to $100 million per offence.
Surprise joint communiqué signals provisional maritime security agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz; Brent crude drops ~$20 from peak toward $101/bbl.
Fuel excise halved — consumers to see ~26 cpl relief at the pump
Australian fuel excise halved from 52.6 to 26.3 cpl for three months (1 Apr – 30 Jun) following National Cabinet decision on 30 March.
Brent crude pulls back from $119 peak amid preliminary ceasefire signals; market cautious
Brent crude surges 8% to settle at USD 109.03 after Trump national address warns of further military action against Iran over next two to three weeks
Dated Brent (physical oil) surges above USD 141 — highest since 2008 — as Strait of Hormuz disruption intensifies
ACCC issues substantiation notices to regional fuel distributors over sizable fuel surcharges and warns of enforcement action against retailers not passing on excise cut
